title: pawia rubra
intro: Pawia rubra, also known as the Red Coral Tree, is a tropical plant species native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in Fiji and other parts of Oceania. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Pawia rubra include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a natural remedy for wounds and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into a tea or infusion, which can be used to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
